Dr. Alejandro Colman-Lerner

personal site

B.A., Biology, University of Buenos Aires, 1992.

Ph.D. Biology, University of Buenos Aires, 1997.

Thesis title "Regulation of Ovarian Follicular Growth and Maturation by Growth Factors and Extracellular Matrix Components."

Joined the Institute in 1998.

Current research projects include studies of cell polarity establishment and signal transduction of S.cerevisiae, experiments to support computational modeling of the mating pheromone response in S.cerevisiae use of aptamer libraries to probe complex mammalian phenotypes and to create an array of affinity reagents to study the S.cerevisiae proteome.

Return to the Molecular Sciences Institute People Page
